free_text: Study of the Nuclear Reactions Sc45(p,n)Ti45,
Cu63(p,n)Zn63, Cu65(p,n)Zn65, and Zn(p,n)Ga
BP
The target used was 120-keV thick to protons of 2.9
MeV and had a tungsten backing.
Counter method involves the use of two BF3 counters,
one embedded in a paragon cylinder 5 in. in diameter
and 5 in. in length (modified long counter) and the
second surrounded by a ring of paraffin 0.5 in. thick
and 2 in. in length (slow counter). Both counters
were placed at 0 degree with respect to the direction
of the proton beam and subtended approximately the
same solid angle at the target.
The target used was 120-keV thick to protons of 2.9
MeV and had a tungsten backing.
The yield of neutrons measured by the slow counter
from threshold to 0.070 MeV above threshold.
The yield has been corrected for background.
The separated isotope of Cu63
(99.11 percent Cu63) in the form of CuO was obtained
from the Oak Ridge National Laboratory. The CuO was
reduced to metallic copper and targets were made by
evaporating the metal onto weighed aluminum blanks or
onto tungsten blanks.

The separated isotope of Cu65
(98.2 percent Cu65) in the form of CuO was reduced
to metallic copper and evaporated onto weighed
aluminum disks.
